subject: Math
title: Coordinate Plane & Geometry
description: Plotting points, quadrants, and shapes on a coordinate grid.

questions.q: What are the coordinates of a point 3 right and 5 up from the origin?
questions.explanation: Right = positive x, Up = positive y → (3, 5).

questions.q: A point at (4, 0) lies on the ___ axis.
questions.explanation: When y = 0, the point is on the x-axis.

questions.q: In which quadrant is the point (–3, 4)?
questions.explanation: Negative x, positive y = Quadrant II.

questions.q: A rectangle has corners at (1,1), (4,1), (4,3), (1,3). What is its area?
What is the distance between (2,5) and (2,9)?
Which point is in Quadrant IV?
questions.explanation: Positive x, negative y = Quadrant IV.

questions.q: If you reflect point (3, 7) across the x-axis, what are the new coordinates?
questions.explanation: Reflecting across the x-axis changes y to –y: (3, –7).

questions.q: What is the perimeter of a square with corners at (0,0), (4,0), (4,4), (0,4)?
Point A is at (–3, –3). In which quadrant does it lie?
questions.explanation: Negative x, negative y = Quadrant III.
